key_insight: A self-supervised, mask-aware Transformer can learn FBG force estimation that remains robust when sensor channels are missing or fractured.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable force sensing for catheter-scale surgical robotics, where tight geometries make FBG channel dropouts and nonlinear cross-axis coupling hard to avoid.
method: Pretrain the Transformer with masked-channel reconstruction on unlabeled sensor streams to learn channel relationships.
Fine-tune with a balanced clean-and-corrupted-view regression loss plus a dynamic corruption curriculum.
Add a heteroscedastic uncertainty head to predict per-axis confidence in one forward pass.
evidence_signal: On an 8-channel catheter-scale FBG dataset, the model reached 0.0066 N RMSE nominally and 0.0126 N under severe 4-channel loss, outperforming a 255-model bank (0.0154 N at 4-channel loss).
watch_item: Performance depends on representative corruption patterns and careful training; unseen failure modes or different catheter/sensor layouts may require additional adaptation.
paper_title: Self-Supervised Mask-Aware Transformers for Fault-Tolerant FBG Force Sensing in Minimally Invasive Surgical Robotics
